hero.question: Will Rachida Dati win the Paris mayor election?
hero.prob_take: At 20.5%, the crowd is pricing Dati as a clear underdog despite her surging momentum, which means you're getting 4-to-1 odds on a candidate who just moved 4.5 points in 24 hours on real political momentum. The +1.1M in volume spike suggests smart money is finally noticing what Paris insiders already knew: Dati's Renaissance backing and tough-on-crime platform are resonating with centrist voters who will actually show up to vote.

daily_take.headline: Dati's Paris Surge Signals Conservative Momentum, But 20% Still Feels Generous
daily_take.deck: Rachida Dati jumped 4.5 points to 20.5% overnight on fresh polling showing traction with right-wing voters ahead of Paris' mayoral race. The move makes sense, but smart money shouldn't chase. At 1 in 5 odds, the market is pricing in a competitive three-way race when the structural advantage still belongs to the incumbent. Wait for a pullback before buying.

sidebar.headline: Steyer's California resurrection looks overcooked at 8.2%, especially after a 4.2-point spike that smells like Reddit money.
sidebar.label: Tom Steyer 2026: 8.2%

sidebar.headline: Arizona's tournament odds jumped hard, but a mid-major program hitting 19.5% demands proof of elite recruiting before you follow the crowd.
sidebar.label: Arizona NCAA 2026: 19.5%

sidebar.headline: Anduril's IPO odds just crashed 22 points. Defense contractors don't go public on a whim, so either the timeline shifted or someone got nervous.
sidebar.label: Anduril IPO: 19.0%
